title: 341. Flatten Nested List Iterator
question:
Given a nested list of integers, implement an iterator to flatten it.

Each element is either an integer, or a list -- whose elements may also be integers or other lists.

Example 1:

Input: [[1,1],2,[1,1]]
Output: [1,1,2,1,1]
Explanation: By calling next repeatedly until hasNext returns false, 
             the order of elements returned by next should be: [1,1,2,1,1].

Example 2:

Input: [1,[4,[6]]]
Output: [1,4,6]
Explanation: By calling next repeatedly until hasNext returns false, 
             the order of elements returned by next should be: [1,4,6].
code: # \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A# This is the interface that allows for creating nested lists.\u000A# You should not implement it, or speculate about its implementation\u000A# \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A#class NestedInteger(object):\u000A#    def isInteger(self):\u000A#        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A#        @return True if this NestedInteger holds a single integer, rather than a nested list.\u000A#        :rtype bool\u000A#        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A#\u000A#    def getInteger(self):\u000A#        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A#        @return the single integer that this NestedInteger holds, if it holds a single integer\u000A#        Return None if this NestedInteger holds a nested list\u000A#        :rtype int\u000A#        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A#\u000A#    def getList(self):\u000A#        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A#        @return the nested list that this NestedInteger holds, if it holds a nested list\u000A#        Return None if this NestedInteger holds a single integer\u000A#        :rtype List[NestedInteger]\u000A#        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A\u000Aclass NestedIterator(object):\u000A\u000A    def __init__(self, nestedList):\u000A        self.stack, self.cache \u003D [[nestedList, 0]], None\u000A\u000A    def next(self):\u000A        self.hasNext()\u000A        res, self.cache \u003D self.cache, None\u000A        return res\u000A    \u000A\u000A    def hasNext(self):\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        :rtype: bool\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        if self.cache is not None: return True\u000A        elif not self.stack: return False\u000A        next_lst, next_idx \u003D self.stack[\u002D1]\u000A        if next_idx \u003C len(next_lst):\u000A            if next_lst[next_idx].isInteger():\u000A                self.cache \u003D next_lst[next_idx].getInteger()\u000A                self.stack[\u002D1][1] +\u003D 1\u000A                return True\u000A            else:\u000A                self.stack[\u002D1][1] +\u003D 1\u000A                self.stack.append([next_lst[next_idx].getList(), 0])\u000A                return self.hasNext()\u000A        else:\u000A            self.stack.pop()\u000A            return self.hasNext()\u000A    \u000A\u000A# Your NestedIterator object will be instantiated and called as such:\u000A# i, v \u003D NestedIterator(nestedList), []\u000A# while i.hasNext(): v.append(i.next())
